video_title: Untold: The Fall of Favre | Official Trailer | Netflix
comment_text: I watched this because Brett Favre was someone that I idolized over every other football player growing up.  I also knew that it was going to challenge my opinion of him because of what I knew from the Internet and news clips reported.  That said, shame on the Black people in the documentary that continued to point out that if he wasn’t white, things would go differently for him.   Especially shame on Shannon Sharpe for basically saying everyone blames Black people for stuff like this, but if you really look into it, all of these things are done by white people. That was inaccurate and extremely unnecessary and shame on Netflix for allowing that to be in the documentary. Anything Brett did is not defensible but why put such a racist ridiculous comment in the documentary? This isn’t about race it’s about greed and it’s an eye-opening look at what your hero is really like if you ever really get to know him. That’s it. It doesn’t change radically high statistics because a guy like this does something like this.  It just makes you feel sorry for his family, especially his wife and daughter because the embarrassment and the failure fell all over them while everyone like me still tries to find a way to love him for something so insignificant in life as throwing a rubber ball accurately down a stupid field while you are playing a game that has nothing to do with real life.   One thing every football player needs to know is whether or not you were going to be a rocket scientist or a garbage man what you do for money relies solely on fans being interested. It’s a game. It’s a child’s game and children really need to understand that people like athletes and movie stars are meaningless in the scheme of actual world reality.  When their actions or opinions come in to play it’s always a massive mistake for the illusion of what they hope we think about them.  I’m a Tampa Bay boy and when I heard we were getting Tom Brady, I couldn’t care less what kind of dad he was or whether or not I ever got to flip burgers on a Saturday in my backyard with him all I wanted him to do was throw a ball down the field to my favorite receivers so I could enjoy a mindless stupid meaningless sport for a few hours once a week and if he lost, it didn’t bother me in anyway when it was time to go back to being a man on Monday morning.

video_title: Untold: The Fall of Favre | Official Trailer | Netflix
comment_text: Might be worth noting for anyone unfamiliar with the welfare scandal portion, the federal government just asked Mississippi to repay $100,000,000 in misappropriated welfare funds. Of that amount Brett Favre's portion was about $8,000,000 of which he was only asked to repay the $1,000,000 he was paid for HHS advertisements he never recorded. The $1,000,000 was needed to finish up the volleyball facility but the grants from un-paidout welfare funds had dried up so this was the workaround they came up with. Favre was only asked to repay the $1,000,000, not any of the other funds, and even then still refuses to pay back interest on the $1,000,000 he stole. The investigative reporter that uncovered most of this is Anna Wolfe of Mississippi Today which has the complete catalog of her reporting in a section titled The Backchannel for anyone to read up on. Anna Wolfe earned a Pulitzer Prize for her reporting on this story. This story goes far deeper than Favre and several people not protected by fame as he is have gone to jail for it. Many people want to cover it all up though because it touches on how the state of MS denies applicants for TANF funds so the unpaid funds at the end of the year can be rolled over into grants to assist lower income residents of MS with things like job training and such. These grants though are paid out as political favors. The current MS Gov for example was Lt Gov back in this time period and managed to steer a $100,000 grant to his own personal trainer. Just one example of the corruption and how Favre is managing to keep from being charged. It will be interesting to see how far this documentary goes in explaining all this. There was also a drug company Favre represented, shares were offered to the Gov back then, the state invested in the company and nobody knows how much they all made from that when that company was later sold. The corruption goes pretty deep.
